summary: In deep wounds, using chromic gut sutures provides a significant advantage through prolonged strength and resistance against premature breaking down, reducing risks like wound dehiscence that can occur with faster-absorbing materials such as plain gut. The slow dissolution property ensures adequate time for tissue integration without compromising structural integrity needed throughout recovery phases. \boxed{\text{Delayed Absorption}} This summary captures the essence while adhering strictly within two concise statements focused directly on critical aspects related to treatment outcomes in deeper surgical sites requiring robust closure mechanisms post-operation.
